subject: Geology
title: Landslides and Slope Stability
description: A 60-second lesson on Landslides and Slope Stability.

hook.text: A hillside looks solid until the rain comes. Saturated soil loses its grip and an entire slope can collapse.

buildup.text: Landslides occur when gravity overcomes the strength holding soil or rock on a slope. Water is the main trigger.

discovery.text: Clay layers act as slip surfaces. When wet, clay becomes slippery and lets entire blocks of land slide downhill.

twist.text: Human activity increases landslide risk. Road cuts, deforestation, and poor drainage destabilize slopes.

climax.text: Landslide hazard maps save lives by showing where slopes are most vulnerable. Geology identifies danger zones.

punchline.text: Gravity never forgets a weak slope.

quiz.question: What is the most common trigger for landslides?
